thag4.10:1.1: The Dhamma protects those who live by the Dhamma.
thag4.10:1.3: This—the reward when the Dhamma’s well-practiced:
thag4.10:1.4: One who lives by the Dhamma doesn’t go to a bad destination.
thag4.10:3.1: So you should engender desire for acts of Dhamma,
thag4.10:3.2: rejoicing in the One Well-Gone, the one who is Such.
thag4.10:3.3: Standing firm in the Dhamma, of the foremost One Well-Gone, his disciples are guided
thag4.10:4.1: Burst is the root of the boil;
thag4.10:4.3: He, having ended his wandering-on, has no stain—
thag4.10:4.4: like the moon on a clear full-moon night.
